data.title: Rio Tinto may buy Glencore to form mining leader with copper prices near record
data.description:
By Steve Goldstein

World's biggest miner would be created

Swiss mining and commodity trading giant Glencore said early on January 9, 2026, it is in merger talks with British-Australian rival Rio Tinto.

Rio Tinto may buy Glencore to create the biggest mining company, both companies said on Friday, in a deal that comes as metals prices have soared.

Both companies, in brief statements, say they are in talks on a merger that could lead to Rio buying shares of Glencore in an all-stock deal. Rio Tinto will have until Feb. 5 to make an offer under U.K. takeover rules.

Rio Tinto (UK:RIO) (RIO) closed Thursday with a market cap of $148 billion, and Glencore (UK:GLEN) at GBP48 billion ($64 billion).

Glencore shares shot up 10% in London trade, while Rio Tinto stock fell 2%.

The two if combined would move ahead of BHP Group (BHP), which on Thursday had a market cap of $161 billion.

The front-month copper contract (HG00) has soared 36% over the last 52 weeks, as a range of metals have increased in value on demand for artificial-intelligence and electrification.

The two companies have previously held merger talks.

"After last talks failed in late 2024, Glencore's decision to restructure its coal assets under one unit in Australia in mid-2025 was a clear signal that the Swiss giant was preparing to be ready for a merger with Rio," said analysts at Alphavalue, a research service.

"Also, with BHP on weak footing - after various failed Anglo American takeover attempts, which may perhaps trigger search for a new 'external' CEO, Rio-Glencore merger talks couldn't have happened at a better time."

Analysts at Cowen said a combined company would be a leader in iron ore, copper, aluminum, lithium, cobalt, nickel, zinc, thermal coal and steel-making coal.

Bank of America analysts said Anglo American's (UK:AAL) deal to buy Teck Resources (TECK) created something of a "now or never" moment and said the big question is how BHP responds.

-Steve Goldstein

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  01-09-26 0600ET


Copyright (c) 2026 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: We paid my daughter and son-in-law's mortgage when it reached $76K. Here's why we chose that magic figure.
data.description:
By Quentin Fottrell

'My husband, daughter, son-in-law and I all went to the bank'

"Do I need to fill out a Form 709 for this on my taxes this year?" (Photo subject is a model.)

Dear Quentin,

Last year, I told my daughter and her husband that I would pay off their mortgage if they got it under $76,000. My husband, daughter, son-in-law and I all went to the bank. My wife wrote the check for the balance.

Here's why we chose $76,000: Both my husband and I plan to each give $19,000 to both our daughter and son-in-law. Do I need to fill out a Form 709 for this on my taxes this year? Was this a good idea?

The Parent

Don't miss: 'You need a third job or a rich husband': I fell victim to Allen Stanford's multibillion-dollar Ponzi scheme and survived

You can email The Moneyist with any financial and ethical questions at qfottrell@marketwatch.com. The Moneyist regrets he cannot reply to questions individually.

"My wife wrote the check for the balance." (Photo subject is a model.)

Dear Parent,

You may start a trend among wealthy parents, making $76,000 the magic number.

If you and your husband give $76,000 to your daughter and your son-in-law -both of you giving $19,000 to each separately - you are exactly within the federal annual gift-tax exemption. It does not matter whether you pay this money from a joint account or a separate account; the Internal Revenue Service bases these gifts on donor to recipient.

To answer your question: File an IRS Form 709 even though the total value of your gifts to your daughter and her husband was $19,000 or less during the 2025 calendar year; the exemption applies as long as the giftees have immediate and unrestricted right to use, possess or enjoy the gift. This amount is the annual gift tax exclusion for 2025 and 2026 remains at $19,000.

The gift-splitting election on Form 709 is, for you, an opportunity to clarify this gift, not a burden.

Your wife writing the check will not cause confusion for the IRS, as long as you fill out your Form 709. As your wife wrote the check, the IRS might initially treat the full mortgage payoff as 100% her gift. If that total exceeded her annual exclusion when combined for both recipients, you both should fill out a Form 709. Normally, it would not be necessary given the amount.

By electing gift splitting on Form 709, however, your gift is treated as having been made 50% by you and 50% by your wife, regardless of whose check was used. This tallies with your original intent, keeping your and your wife's share within the annual gift-tax exemption. The gift-splitting election on Form 709 is, for you, an opportunity to clarify this gift, not a burden.

You could also have given your daughter and her husband $152,000 (by giving $76,000 on Dec. 31 and again on Jan. 1). For them, there will be no better feeling than paying off their mortgage and writing that last check - even if the satisfaction is slightly dulled because of your generosity.

There's an argument for and against such gifts, and some critics would say that you are lessening the requirement and satisfaction of doing the hard work by removing such obstacles from your children's lives with one trip to the bank. Others might say you've freed them up to save for retirement earlier and enjoy their lives free from such burdens.

Remember, if your daughter's marriage breaks down, there's no clawing back that gift.

The only question that's important here is the one you ask. On this occasion, it's a question related to the IRS. It's also your money, and you have earned the right to use it in a way that maximizes the positive impact for your loved ones, which, of course, gives you pleasure too. (The lifetime estate tax is $15 million for 2026, so I'm assuming you are well below that.)

Was it a good idea? Yes, it was a great idea, especially for your daughter and her husband. Remember, if your daughter's marriage breaks down, there's no clawing back that gift (unless your son-in-law agreed to it voluntarily). As long as you and your wife have enough set aside for retirement, keep living the dream and passing it along to your nearest and dearest.

Mortgage payments are, after all, one of the heaviest weights people carry.

Don't miss: 'We track our finances religiously': Are we obliged to pay for our daughter's medical school? We've $2.6 million saved for retirement.

More columns from Quentin Fottrell:

'He has become hostile': My mom is in her 80s and has dementia. My brother is blocking access.

I'm afraid my financial adviser will steal my money. I've read too many cautionary tales. How can I be sure?

'He even bragged about his Mercedes-Benz:' I rejected two egotistical advisers. I managed my life savings and they tripled over 25 years. Did I do OK?

'I have absolutely no idea what to do': I was diagnosed with Alzheimer's disease and inherited $400K. How do I invest it?

Check out the Moneyist private Facebook group, where we look for answers to life's thorniest money issues. Post your questions or weigh in on the latest Moneyist columns.

By submitting your story to Dow Jones & Co., the publisher of MarketWatch, you understand and agree that we may use your story, or versions of it, in all media and platforms.

-Quentin Fottrell

data.title: China Auto Sales Slumped in December Amid Slowing Demand
data.description:
By Jiahui Huang 




  China's auto sales fell last month as the country continued to grapple with slowing demand, a sign that business conditions could remain challenging in 2026 for the world's largest car market. 



  Retail sales of passenger cars dropped 14% to 2.26 million units in December from a year earlier, the China Passenger Car Association said Friday. Sales rose 1.6% from November. 



  For the full year, retail sales of passenger cars in China rose 3.8% to 23.7 million units. 



  The CPCA said December's weak performance was due to consumers taking a wait-and-see approach, as the government's trade-in subsidies were set to expire by the end of the year. Beijing late last month renewed the car trade-in program for 2026 as part of a broader effort to boost consumption. 



  China sold more electric vehicles than gasoline-powered cars last year. Retail sales of new-energy cars, a category that includes battery EVs and plug-in hybrids, accounted for 57% of passenger-vehicle sales in 2025, the association said. 



  Retail sales of NEVs rose 2.6% to 1.3 million units in December, bringing the annual figure to 12.8 million units, an 18% increase. 



  The growth momentum of car exports remained strong as automakers, faced with a saturated domestic market, continued their aggressive expansion abroad. Exports of passenger cars jumped 46% last month from a year earlier to 588,000 units, CPCA data showed. 



  Tesla delivered 97,171 cars made at its Shanghai plant to Chinese buyers, and exported 3,328 units. 



  The near-term outlook for the world's largest auto market is encouraging, however, as Chinese carmakers and dealers usually work hard to ensure a strong start to the year. 



  The CPCA said China's auto market is set for a cautious but stable start to 2026, thanks to early policy support, pent-up demand and a late Lunar New Year in mid-February. It noted a pattern of strong buying by migrant workers and first-time buyers before the Lunar New Year holidays, which will likely help boost January sales. 



  China's auto-market sales is likely to follow a U-shaped trajectory this year, with a strong growth phase early in the year, followed by a midyear slowdown, before a rebound toward the year-end, the CPCA said. Overall vehicle sales in the domestic market are expected to be broadly flat from 2025, while exports will likely maintain double-digit growth of more than 10%, it said. 





  Write to Jiahui Huang at jiahui.huang@wsj.com 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  January 09, 2026 05:41 ET (10:41 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2026 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Univest Securities and CEO Edric Yi Guo Bridge Capital Markets and Education Through Global Engagement
data.description:
NEW YORK CITY, NY / ACCESS Newswire / January 9, 2026 / Univest Securities and its Chief Executive Officer, Edric Yi Guo, are expanding global engagement between Asia and the United States through active participation in capital markets forums and international education initiatives, reflecting the firm's broader commitment to cross-border collaboration and public-interest dialogue.

With Univest Securities' extensive presence in the Asia-Pacific region and North America, Mr. Guo works closely with companies as they navigate complex regulatory environments and capital market structures. In particular, he has expertise in capital markets advisory, private placements, as well as public offerings, serving both institutional and retail investors.

In the Asia Go IPO Summit, co-hosted by Nasdaq and Marcum Asia CPAs LLP, Univest Securities participated in discussions between investment banks, regulators, legal advisors, accounting firms, and institutional investors about regulatory, structural, and market considerations for Asian companies seeking U.S. public listings. As part of the summit, Mr. Guo joined industry leaders in panel discussions centered on investor expectations and market readiness, with Univest contributing to the broader dialogue around Asia-U.S. capital market access.

Furthermore, education is also a central focus of Mr. Guo's philanthropic work. Specifically, he has made a personal donation of RMB 10 million (approximately USD 1.4 million) to Shanghai Jiao Tong University, becoming the largest individual donor in the university's history. This contribution contributes to the development of long-term academic initiatives, including those that promote interdisciplinary research and innovation, as well as the development of future talent. In addition to strengthening Chinese education and research capacity, the gift contributes to broader knowledge exchange and global social advancement.

Mr. Guo's commitment to education further extends through his role as Vice Chairman of the Forum for World Education (FWE), a nonprofit organization dedicated to advancing education as a platform for diplomacy, peace, and international cooperation. The objective of FWE's forums and initiatives is to bring together professionals from education, policy, industry, and academia to examine how education can serve as a platform for cross-cultural understanding and collaboration.

The Education for Global Diplomacy and Peace Conference-also known as the New York Summit-brought together policymakers, business leaders, and university scholars, with Mr. Guo among the attendees. In particular, the summit explored how international education can address contemporary global challenges and support more constructive cross-border engagement. In addition, there were discussions on education as diplomacy, youth development, sustainability-oriented collaboration, and opportunities for academic study, research exchange, and industry engagement between Asia and the United States.

Taken together, the capital markets activities and education-focused nonprofit initiatives involving Univest Securities and Mr. Guo reinforces a sustained commitment to broadening the impact of financial practice beyond transactional outcomes, fostering long-term global engagement, and contributing to lasting public benefit.

About Univest Securities, LLC

Registered with FINRA since 1994, Univest Securities, LLC provides a broad range of financial services to institutional and retail clients globally, including brokerage and execution services, sales and trading, market making, investment banking and advisory, and wealth management. Leveraging its geographic roots across Asia Pacific and North America, Univest supports small-to-medium-sized public and private enterprises through its Investment Banking and Corporate Financing Division, offering services such as mergers and acquisitions advisory, capital markets advisory, valuation and fairness opinions, private placements, and initial and secondary public offerings. Since 2019, Univest has raised over $1.7 billion in capital for issuers worldwide and completed approximately 100 transactions across multiple industries.

For more information, please visit www.univest.us.

Media Contact:

Univest Securities, LLC
75 Rockefeller Plaza, Suite 18C
New York, NY 10019
Phone: (212) 343-8888
Email: info@univest.us

SOURCE : Univest Securities



View the original press release on ACCESS Newswire

data.title: 'I'm at a crossroads': I have $35K left on my mortgage at 3% interest. Do I dip into my $700K IRA to pay it off?
data.description:
By Quentin Fottrell

'Over the last year, my two retirement accounts averaged a 15% gain'

"It would be nice not to have to pay the $1,350 each month." (Photo subject is a model.)

Dear Quentin,

Is it smart to pay off my house?

I am 61 years old and have $50,000 in a 401(k) and $700,000 in a traditional IRA. There is $35,000 left on the house at 3% interest, and my monthly mortgage and escrow payment is $1,350.

Over the last year, my two retirement accounts averaged a 15% gain in the market. (I also have a $250,000 annuity, but I don't think I should count that as part of this decision.)

It would be nice not to have to pay the $1,350 each month. If I do, which account should I take the money from - the IRA or the 401(k)? I'm at a crossroads.

Should I pay off the house?

Nearing Retirement

You can email The Moneyist with any financial and ethical questions at qfottrell@marketwatch.com, and follow Quentin Fottrell on X, the platform formerly known as Twitter.

Don't miss: 'I'm terrified I'll be homeless when my husband, 76, stops working': We only have $100K. What happens to people like us?

Your 3% interest rate is just about in line with inflation, and you are putting the rest of your funds to much better work with the 15% return on your investments.

Dear Nearing,

All roads are pointing to the Emerald City.

Given the sum and interest rate involved, I'm inclined to leave your mortgage alone.

That said, I understand that finance is far more than just interest rates and dollar signs; it's also about peace of mind and the joy of living in a home that you own completely. Plus, you get to use that extra cash for other things, including - I hope - investing in your retirement.

Look at this way: Your 3% interest rate is just about in line with inflation, and you are putting the rest of your funds to much better work with the 15% return on your investments. Sure, we will all have down years. The S&P 500 SPX fell by 18% in 2022, but it has since more than recovered.

Withdrawals from a traditional 401(k) or IRA are considered ordinary income and are subject to your federal and, depending on where you live, state income tax rate. For a Roth IRA or Roth 401(k), withdrawals of contributions are tax-free.

Earnings are also tax-free and penalty-free if the account has been open for at least five years and you are over age 59 1/2, which you are. For that reason, you don't have to worry about paying extra fees. This is, for the most part, a luxury withdrawal.

The decision to pay off the house is yours, but you'll also have to take considerably more than $35,000 out of your retirement accounts, assuming that you need to pay tax on these. If you're in the 22% tax bracket, you'd have to take out closer to $45,000 to get that cash.

Higher tax bracket

For anyone at risk of pushing themselves into a higher tax bracket, it makes sense to withdraw some of that $45,000 in December, and the rest in January. Assuming you did not do that, you could take a smaller sum and overpay your mortgage.

If you decide to pay off your mortgage, consider taking the money from your IRA. Otherwise, you'd be effectively liquidating your 401(k). While 401(k)s can be more restrictive with investment choices, they also come with other benefits, such as employer matches (free money).

A member of the Moneyist Facebook Group, which mostly favors keeping the mortgage, has a compromise: "I would just accelerate your payments," they say. "Add enough to your payment every month that goes to principal to pay it off in a time frame like 24 or 36 months."

"By the time you're retired it's paid off but you're not taking a big lump sum out of any of your accounts," they add. "I overpay starting from day one when I have a mortgage or a car payment to accelerate payoff." You will also benefit psychologically from speeding up your progress.

It would be folly not to look at the opportunity cost of taking that $45,000 from your IRA. Over a 15-year period, that could cost you roughly $79,000 in today's dollars, assuming a 7% real return, or about $143,000 using the S&P 500's long-term average of around 10%.

Accelerating payments

Those figures, while sobering, reflect only the investment growth you give up by removing the money; they do not account for additional costs such as taxes on the withdrawal, or the impact of losing tax-deferred compounding.

Another Moneyist reader adds: "My house will be paid off in a couple of years, so I won't have a mortgage payment anymore. I will still have property taxes, maintenance, etc., but as I retire, that reduction in monthly expenses will allow me to live on a lower income."

"This ties into many people's concept of 'lifestyle,'" they add. "If someone is used to leasing cars, renting, and paying for subscription services - essentially a lifestyle of both needs and wants - then having a mortgage in retirement may be a non-issue."

"If, however, you base your lifestyle more on 'needs' and 'minimum costs,' then having the mortgage paid off becomes a higher priority. Your income while working, your income in retirement, and your overall cost of living all play a role in this as well."

OK, friend. Decision time. It seems important that you pay this off. It will set you psychologically free after years of paying your mortgage. If so, take the money from your traditional IRA and, ideally, spread the withdrawals over two (or more) tax years.

If this pushes you into a higher tax bracket, however, think again about paying it off. If all roads point to the Emerald City, where you are free of debt, take the one of least resistance.

A 3% interest rate is the kind of flying monkey that most people would want on their tail.

Related: 'How do I shield my retirement savings?' I'm worried about Trump's trade war and Fed's willingness to cut interest rates

The Moneyist regrets he cannot reply to questions individually.

More columns from Quentin Fottrell:

My therapist said he was in-network. He's not. Am I obliged to pay out-of-network rates for all prior sessions?

Can my husband contest his late brother's $600K will? He experienced oxygen deprivation due to COPD before he died.

I have early Alzheimer's and my husband has stage 4 kidney disease. We just inherited $50K. How can this help us?

Check out the Moneyist private Facebook group, where we look for answers to life's thorniest money issues. Post your questions or weigh in on the latest Moneyist columns.

By submitting your story to Dow Jones & Co., the publisher of MarketWatch, you understand and agree that we may use your story, or versions of it, in all media and platforms.

-Quentin Fottrell

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  01-09-26 0525ET

data.title: Glencore Shares Jump on Talks With Rio Tinto to Create World's Largest Miner
data.description:
By Adam Whittaker 




  Shares in commodity giant Glencore climbed on talks with Rio Tinto for an all-share merger that would create the world's largest mining company. 



  In morning trade in London, Glencore shares rose 8.5% to 448 pence--their highest level in over a year. Despite the rise, shares still trade significantly below their peak in January 2023, and give the Switzerland-based commodity trading and mining group a market capitalization of roughly $70.43 billion. 



  As merger-and-acquisition activity across the mining sector heats up, Glencore has been viewed by some analysts as a potential takeover target. 



  Glencore's shares have been weighed down recently in part by its coal division and a weaker copper business relative to companies that focus entirely on mining the red metal. 



  The expectation is that Rio Tinto would buy Glencore, according to the companies. The deal would include a possible combination of some, or all of their businesses. The combined entity would have a market value of more than $200 billion. The companies said the talks were at a preliminary stage. 



  Beyond this, little is known on the structure of any deal. 



  "We would not expect Glencore to get taken out in a nil-premium merger," Jefferies analysts said in a note, referring to the structure of Anglo American's recent deal to buy Canada's Teck in which the bidder didn't offer a premium to combine with the target. 



  Assuming Glencore sells its coal business, the merged entity would likely trade at premium to the weighted average of the two companies, Jefferies analysts added. 



  Copper would be at the heart of the combined business. Rio Tinto wants Glencore's copper growth potential, and exposure to the metal could lift the combined entity's shares, according to analysts. 



  The industrial metal is a core component in wiring and will be central to global electrification efforts. A series of supply shocks at major mines have helped push copper to record highs this week. 



  New mines are expensive to build and mean merger and acquisitions are the quickest and easiest way of securing new supply. The clamor for copper has sparked deal talks between the major miners, and is the driving force behind the Anglo American-Teck Resources deal. 



  Still, a tie-up between Rio Tinto and Glencore isn't guaranteed to happen. The talks revisit a combination that was first discussed around a year ago but broke down. Regulatory hurdles and the future of Glencore's coal and trading units within a combined group could pose hurdles to reaching a deal. 



  There is also the possibility an interloper could emerge. "BHP could look to acquire Glencore with a rival bid," Berenberg's Richard Hatch and Jasper Mainwaring write. 



  BHP--currently the world's No. 1 miner by market value--didn't immediately respond to a request for comment. It previously bid for Anglo American but was rebuffed, and risks falling behind rivals who are snapping up copper assets. 



  Glencore has been marketing itself for a sale for some time and is seen a willing seller, RBC Capital Markets analyst Ben Davis wrote in a note to clients. 





  Write to Adam Whittaker at adam.whittaker@wsj.com 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  January 09, 2026 05:30 ET (10:30 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2026 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Huepar Validates the Foundation of its Industry-Leading 5-Year Warranty with Automated Precision Verification
data.description:
Huepar Validates the Foundation of its Industry-Leading 5-Year Warranty with Automated Precision Verification

Huepar Validates the Foundation of its Industry-Leading 5-Year Warranty with Automated Precision Verification

PR Newswire

ZHUHAI, China, Jan. 9, 2026

Global partners witness the rigorous quality systems that power the brand's long-term reliability commitment during the 2025 China Tour.

ZHUHAI, China, Jan. 9, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Huepar, a global leader in high-precision laser levels and measuring tools, recently concluded its strategic milestone event, the "Huepar 2025 China Tour." While titled a tour, this initiative served as a comprehensive "Precision Verification" audit of the company's upgraded quality control systems. The event highlighted the integration of automated precision verification and rigorous stress testing, demonstrating the engineering excellence that underpins Huepar's industry-leading 5-Year Warranty policy, which is currently active across all global sales channels.

As the construction industry undergoes a digital transformation, precision has become the new competitive edge. Huepar's latest initiative demonstrates that high-precision tools are no longer the exclusive domain of legacy brands but are now achievable through advanced, data-driven quality control.

Digital Precision: The Shift to Automated Verification

During the extensive verification process, global industry influencers and key agents witnessed Huepar's strategic transition from manual checking to Automated Precision Verification. This system serves as the first line of defense for quality:

  • Eliminating Human Error: The new automated verification equipment replaces traditional manual reading, utilizing higher sensitivity sensors to eliminate visual errors and ensuring sub-millimeter consistency.
  • Data-Driven Quality: Beyond simple checking, the system integrates intelligent data collection, analyzing precision data from large batches to continuously optimize production parameters.

Rigorous Quality Assurance: The Pillar of the 5-Year Warranty

While automation ensures precision consistency, Huepar's established 5-Year Warranty is justified by a foundation of comprehensive, multi-stage durability testing. The tour showcased the exhaustive protocols that every product must survive:

  • 100% Finished Goods Inspection: Unlike random sampling, Huepar implements a 100% inspection policy for finished goods to ensure zero defects before shipment.
  • Extreme Environment Simulation: Visitors observed the systematic stress tests designed to simulate harsh job site conditions, including High and Low-Temperature Testing, Salt Spray Tests for corrosion resistance, and Vibration/Drop Tests.
  • Lifecycle Endurance: The tour demonstrated the rigorous Aging Tests for laser modules and full units, alongside load testing for components electric base, ensuring long-term stability under heavy use.
  • Packaging Integrity: Comprehensive packaging tests were displayed, ensuring that high-precision instruments remain calibrated even after the rigors of global logistics.

"Our 5-year warranty is not just a marketing promise; it is a calculated commitment backed by data," said Fendy Zhou, Product Director at Huepar. "By combining automated digital verification with our exhaustive torture-testing protocols, we prove to our users why Huepar tools are built to last."

2026 Strategic Outlook: Service and Sustainability

With product reliability validated, Huepar announced its core strategic focus for 2026 to further enhance the user experience:

  • Global Service Infrastructure: To support the 5-year warranty ecosystem, Huepar is expanding dedicated repair and service stations in the United States, Europe, and the United Kingdom, ensuring faster turnaround times and localized support.
  • Sustainability Initiative: In a move to minimize environmental impact, Huepar is transitioning to comprehensive electronic manuals, significantly reducing paper waste across its global supply chain.

Transparency in Action

The verification tour culminated in a unique "Hands-on Calibration" session. Under the guidance of technical specialists, global partners personally calibrated devices, gaining a granular understanding of the accuracy Huepar tools deliver. This transparent approach reinforces the long-term relationships Huepar aims to build with its global community.

About Huepar

Huepar is a professional brand committed to providing high-precision laser measuring tools for engineers, designers, and DIY enthusiasts worldwide. Integrating advanced technology with user-centric design, Huepar continues to drive the industry forward with tools that are accurate, durable, and accessible. Its mission is to empower professionals with precision tools that enhance efficiency, with a focus on product innovation and durability.

For more information, please visit https://huepar.com or follow us on social media.

Media Contact: Huepar Marketing Team, marketing@huepar.com

SOURCE Huepar

data.title: Kia Unveils EV2 at Brussels Motor Show
data.description:
Kia Unveils EV2 at Brussels Motor Show

Kia Unveils EV2 at Brussels Motor Show

PR Newswire

BRUSSELS and SEOUL, South Korea, Jan. 9, 2026
  • The Kia EV2 – the brand's sixth dedicated electric model – debuts at the 2026 Brussels Motor Show
  • Compact B-SUV designed to support diverse modern lifestyles, delivering class-above interior space and an emotionally engaging electric driving experience
  • Up to 448 km driving range, EV Route Planner, Plug & Charge and bidirectional V2L
  • EV3 GT, EV4 Hatchback GT and EV5 GT also revealed, expanding Kia's electric performance portfolio

BRUSSELS and SEOUL, South Korea, Jan. 9, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Kia Corporation today unveiled the EV2 at the 2026 Brussels Motor Show, marking the global premiere of a compact electric SUV designed to make electric mobility more emotional, intuitive and accessible.

"With the Kia EV2, we are expanding the reach of Kia's electric mobility to customers who want a compact car that still feels spacious, capable and emotionally rewarding. EV2 reflects our commitment to practical innovation — bringing advanced EV technology, thoughtful design and everyday usability into a model created for the way people live and move in cities around the world." — Ho Sung Song, President and CEO of Kia

Alongside the EV2, Kia also revealed the EV2 GT Line and introduced the latest electric GT models — EV3 GT, EV4 Hatchback GT and EV5 GT — underscoring the brand's commitment to both accessible electrification and engaging performance.

EV2: A New Emotional Gateway to Electric Mobility

The EV2 plays a strategic role within Kia's expanding electric vehicle (EV) portfolio. As the most compact electric SUV in the lineup, it is designed as a gateway model for first-time EV drivers and new Kia buyers, while remaining versatile enough to serve as a primary household vehicle — easy to live with, yet emotionally rewarding.

Kia's new compact SUV delivers an experience beyond its class thanks to:

  • A calm, welcoming cabin atmosphere
  • Class-above interior space and flexibility
  • Easy, confidence-inspiring urban driving dynamics

Production and Launch Timing

Production of the EV2 will begin in the first quarter of 2026 with the standard-range model, followed by long-range and GT Line variants throughout the year. Market launch timings will be announced closer to the start of sales.

The EV3 GT, EV4 Hatchback GT and EV5 GT are scheduled to enter production in the second half of 2026.

For more information, visit the Kia Global Media Center for more.

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/kia-unveils-ev2-at-brussels-motor-show-302657196.html

SOURCE Kia Corporation
